title: genista insularis subsp. insularis
intro: Genista insularis subsp. insularis, also known as the Madeiran broom or Madeiran genista, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and thorny branches. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing anxiety and stress levels, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in the treatment of various digestive issues. The plant can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, the flowers and leaves of the Genista insularis subsp. insularis plant have been used in traditional herbal preparations to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
